gold_root: a DNS Planner/Enactor race published an empty DNS record for the regional DynamoDB endpoint, so DynamoDB became unresolvable and the AWS control services that depend on it were impaired — the EC2/NLB/app 5xx are DOWNSTREAM victims. Fix the DNS state first, then drain the control-plane backlog.
red_herrings: the EC2/NLB/app-frontend victims being the root cause | scaling or failing over the loud victims will help | a bad deploy | a raw capacity shortage

red_herrings: a pure caching-DNS bug | re-running the DNS deploy will fix it | a fileserver hardware fault | a bad application deploy
stated_cause: the service is just overloaded and needs more replicas

gold_root: the final RENAME of a large migration deadlocked the InnoDB semaphore so the read 'replica-pool' entered a crash-recovery loop; ADDING capacity feeds new replicas into the same loop — pull traffic and let the RENAME finish
red_herrings: a plain capacity shortage | adding/promoting read replicas will help | a bad deploy | a network fault

gold_root: a 43-second intersite link loss made the Raft 'orchestrator' deselect the East primary and reform quorum on the West coast; the database 'problem' is a network partition / split-brain, and a naive failback would lose ~40min of writes
red_herrings: a database capacity problem | failing back to East is safe | promoting more read replicas helps | a bad deploy

gold_root: systemd silently clamped LimitNOFILE so 'proxysql' hit a 65k FD ceiling far below its config; connection saturation stalls writes on mysql1 — it looks like DB capacity but is a silent FD limit
red_herrings: a database capacity / connection-pool shortage | a resource-intensive query is to blame | restarting ProxySQL will fix it | the primary promotion caused it

gold_root: two ZooKeeper clusters ('zk-ensemble') ended up with conflicting Kafka state after a partition, so coordination reads diverged (split-brain); reconciling the ensemble / fencing the stale quorum is the fix, not restarting Kafka or adding brokers
red_herrings: a Kafka broker capacity shortage | restarting the brokers will fix it | a bad deploy | a disk problem on the brokers

gold_root: the external 'gcp-network-api' was revoked by a GCP account-suspension sweep, so Railway's control plane lost its network API — an external dependency revocation, not a Railway bug. Cached routing state masks it until it expires; reinstate the API, don't just restart (a restart rebuilds from the revoked API).
red_herrings: a bug in the railway-control-plane code | restarting the control plane will fix it | a bad deploy | internal resource exhaustion
